title: Do Kegel Exercises Really Work?
body: Really solid info here. I’d just add — if anyone’s doing kegels and still struggling, it’s worth exploring the nervous system angle too. Breath control, reverse kegels, and structured awareness training (like in Secrets of the First Time by Dr. Jason Langford) helped me feel control I didn’t know was possible.
author: @secretsofthefirsttime

title: DAY IN THE LIFE OF A UNIVERSITY STUDENT // KU LEUVEN// ベルギーの大学生Youtuberの1日に密着　（字幕）
body: I just wanted to quickly point out that this is by no means a representation of what each university student's life in Belgium looks like! Some are way more busy with classes (me too during the first and second year) and have to be at the campus from 9-18! Others have even fewer classes than me or fill in their free time with student associations or sports etc. Also, life will be a bit different at other Belgian universities!
author: @LaurasLifeInJapan

title: DAY IN THE LIFE OF A UNIVERSITY STUDENT // KU LEUVEN// ベルギーの大学生Youtuberの1日に密着　（字幕）

title: DAY IN THE LIFE OF A UNIVERSITY STUDENT // KU LEUVEN// ベルギーの大学生Youtuberの1日に密着　（字幕）
body:
Thank you so much for the very informative video!
I have been enjoying your YouTube channel very much, as I am going to move to Leuven as an exchange student at KU Leuven (the faculty of Arts) this September.

Actually, I am wondering which dormitory I should apply for.

(Should I stay as close as possible to the campus?
Which dormitory is comfortable to live in?
Which dormitory has a good reputation?
Can I apply for some dormitories as the first choice and the second choice?)

I would be very very much grateful if you could tell me some advice on how to choose a dorm.
author: @nanar2482

title: DAY IN THE LIFE OF A UNIVERSITY STUDENT // KU LEUVEN// ベルギーの大学生Youtuberの1日に密着　（字幕）
body: I have a bachelors in Japanese, but I always loved math too. Can you suggest any masters I can get that has math and language?  I was thinking of just getting another bachelors and use my Japanese alongside it. I love learning new languages, just to the point that I can speak and understand people not in an academic way that’s why I want to change it.
